CategoryDescription: Measures leadership support, decision-making autonomy, and the mandate to enforce standards.

Questions.Subcategory: Executive support for decisions through mandates, approval, etc.
Questions.Question: Is there a design critique culture in the organization?
Questions.Description: Organizations with a design critique culture indicates one that has the hard-discussions and has a willingness to share, collaborate, and iterate together.

Questions.Subcategory: Executive support for decisions through mandates, approval, etc.
Questions.Question: Is there leadership support for the design system?
Questions.Description: Support in this context is not necessarily financial, but more in decision-making power and trust to act as subject-matter-experts.

Questions.Subcategory: Permission to make decisions on behalf of the org
Questions.Question: Does the design system team have the autonomy to make decisions for other teams without requiring case-by-case approval?
Questions.Description: Decisions in this case refer to system-level changes, additions, or modifications (ie. not product-level features).

CategoryDescription: Measures the shared understanding of vision, strategy, and values across the organization.

Questions.Subcategory: Alignment on the "why" consumers should use the design system (e.g., the individual contributor value proposition)
Questions.Question: Is there a defined design strategy in the company?
Questions.Description: Does your organization have a cohesive plan on how UX work is done as well as what work is planned.

Questions.Subcategory: Alignment on the "why" consumers should use the design system (e.g., the individual contributor value proposition)
Questions.Question: Do designers at your organisation have deep, T-shaped specialisms (e.g., UX, UI, research, content, information architecture)?
Questions.Description: Some organizations focus on hiring specialists, some generalists.

Questions.Subcategory: Alignment on the "why" consumers should use the design system (e.g., the individual contributor value proposition)
Questions.Question: Does the org have an established digital governance framework?
Questions.Description: Digital governance often takes shape as an alignment team, standards group, design review requirement, or robust QA process, but can come in many forms.

Questions.Subcategory: Alignment on the "why" for the org (vision, charter, future state, needs of the org and/or products)
Questions.Question: Is there a specific goal or mission statement for the design system?
Questions.Description: Does your system have a defined purpose or charter to help frame what success looks like overall.

Questions.Subcategory: Alignment on the "why" for the people doing the work (internal-why)
Questions.Question: Does your system have a specific set of team-level goals or missions that might not directly relate to the org-level mission statement?

Questions.Subcategory: Alignment on the "why" for the people doing the work (internal-why)
Questions.Question: Is there a clear set of values for the design system, specifically?
Questions.Description: Values, in this case, help to inform decision making; e.g. if the system team values accessibility over speed, they may change a priority, etc.

Questions.Subcategory: Alignment on the "why" for the people doing the work (internal-why)
Questions.Question: Is there a clear set of criteria for success for the design system?
Questions.Description: Sometimes this takes the shape of KPIs, quarterly goals, or annual initiatives, etc.

CategoryDescription: Measures how resources (code, design, docs) are born, maintained, and retired. It focuses on the operational reality of the system's assets.

Questions.Subcategory: Resource Creation & Parity
Questions.Question: Does the "Definition of Done" for a new resource require parity across design, code, and documentation?
Questions.Description: Select "Yes" only if a component cannot be officially released until its documentation is also ready.

Questions.Subcategory: Resource Creation & Parity
Questions.Question: Is there a documented process for intake that allows non-maintainers to propose or contribute changes?
Questions.Description: This could include intake forms, contribution guidelines, or open office hours for proposals for individuals that don't directly work on the design system.

Questions.Subcategory: Resource Maintenance
Questions.Question: Are documentation updates tied to the release cadence of the code/design libraries?
Questions.Description: Select "Yes" if your documentation site automatically builds or versions alongside your code packages, even if its a manual process.

Questions.Subcategory: Resource Maintenance
Questions.Question: Is there a clear system for labeling updates so consumers know if a change will break their work?
Questions.Description: Often handled via Semantic Versioning (Major/Minor/Patch), but any clear signaling system counts.

Questions.Subcategory: Deprecation or EOL processes
Questions.Question: When a resource is deprecated, is its status reflected simultaneously in the code and the documentation site?
Questions.Description: This ensures that a resource is marked as deprecated in the documentation the moment it is deprecated in the code.

Questions.Subcategory: Deprecation or EOL processes
Questions.Question: Do you provide migration guides or automated tools to help teams move off deprecated resources?
Questions.Description: Examples include written guides, "codemods", or hands-on support.

CategoryDescription: Measures team composition, resilience to organizational shifts, and capacity to scale.

Questions.Subcategory: Makers: Allocation & Resilience
Questions.Question: Do the individuals maintaining or building the design system split their time across other non-system projects?
Questions.Description: Measures stability of the system team. Splitting time often reduces resilience and slows system delivery.

Questions.Subcategory: Makers: Allocation & Resilience
Questions.Question: Is there a formal agreement or process defining how that split is managed?
Questions.Description: Stability comes from predictable processes, even when allocation is partial.

Questions.Subcategory: Makers: Allocation & Resilience
Questions.Question: Do these maintainers periodically rotate into product teams to build features?
Questions.Description: Rotation can build consumer empathy and reduce the risk of the system becoming disconnected from real product needs.

Questions.Subcategory: Consumers: Allocation & Resilience
Questions.Question: Do the product designers and engineers (consumers) split their time across multiple products or teams simultaneously?
Questions.Description: Measures how fragmented consumer attention is, which can affect adoption quality and feedback loops with the system team.

Questions.Subcategory: Consumers: Allocation & Resilience
Questions.Question: Is there a formal resource planning process for this allocation?
Questions.Description: Formal planning reduces ad-hoc context-switching and helps the system team anticipate consumer bandwidth.

Questions.Subcategory: Consumers: Allocation & Resilience
Questions.Question: Would the organization be open to a rotation program (e.g., embedding consumers into the system team)?
Questions.Description: Rotation programs can improve system adoption, surface real consumer pain points, and strengthen alignment between system and product teams.

Questions.Subcategory: Dedicated (full or partial) core team to work on the design system
Questions.Question: Is there a group of individuals assigned to work on the design system, specifically?
Questions.Description: "Assigned" in this case means a team or set of individuals that are officially asked to work on the design system as a part their role or as their full focus and that work has someone managing it, etc. "Assigned" in this context does NOT mean self-organized or side-of-desk system projects, nor efforts that are not-yet-official in terms of company acceptance.

Questions.Subcategory: Consumers (clear-ish list of who would use the thing you're making)
Questions.Question: Is there a defined group of intended users for the design system?
Questions.Description: Said differently, is the system built for certain internal groups and not others? "Everyone" is not a "defined group".

Questions.Subcategory: Ability to do in house?
Questions.Question: Does the organization have individuals with the skills needed to build a system internally?

Questions.Subcategory: Ability to do in house?
Questions.Question: Can we supplement the core design system team with additional teammates from around the org?
Questions.Description: Supplement, in this case, means contribute to or collaborate with the core design system team.

Questions.Subcategory: Ability to do in house?
Questions.Question: If needed, can the design system team get help?
Questions.Description: This can take place as dedicated resources, temporary project support from a colleague, or even hiring external contractors/an agency.

Questions.Subcategory: Ability to do in house?
Questions.Question: Are there trainings and processes in place to onboard new users to the design system?
Questions.Description: This is referring to onboarding to the design system resources, not the standard employee onboarding; this can take the shape of training documentation, demos, live recordings, etc.

CategoryDescription: Measures the allocation of budget, headcount, and resources for training and long-term sustainability.

Questions.Subcategory: Monetary Investment: Headcount
Questions.Question: Is there leadership approval/commitment for hiring additional teammates?
Questions.Description: Hiring in this case could be net-new, or an internal transfer.

Questions.Subcategory: Long term funding
Questions.Question: Has the organization made an official commitment to fund the ongoing maintenance and longevity of the design system?
Questions.Description: Said differently, will the team that built the system be allocated to help fix bugs, build new features, and deliver quality-of-life enhancements beyond the initial release.

Questions.Subcategory: Monetary Investment: Tools
Questions.Question: Is there a tooling budget for continuing current tools?
Questions.Description: This refers to tools currently utilized by the system team.

Questions.Subcategory: Monetary Investment: Tools
Questions.Question: If the team wanted to explore new or additional tooling, would they be able to?
Questions.Description: Some teams have fixed annual tooling budgets, some are able to add or remove tools adhoc.

Questions.Subcategory: Monetary Investment: Training on courses, teachers, etc
Questions.Question: Does the organization pay for individual trainings, courses, conferences?
Questions.Description: Said differently, are there funds or time allowance for learning and development at the individual level?

Questions.Subcategory: Monetary Investment: Training on courses, teachers, etc
Questions.Question: Does the organization bring in external teachers/speakers/workshop hosts?
Questions.Description: Said differently, are there funds or time allowance for learning and development at the team or group level?

Questions.Subcategory: Time Investment: Training capacity, ability to spend time on learning and not delivery work
Questions.Question: Does the org make space for learning and development in the average work day/week/cycle?

CategoryDescription: Measures the consistency and stability of the tech stack, tooling, and communication platforms.

Questions.Subcategory: Is there a defined tech stack and/or set of frameworks?
Questions.Question: Does your organisation build on multiple platforms?

Questions.Subcategory: Is there a defined tech stack and/or set of frameworks?
Questions.Question: Are each of those platforms building in alignment with the same design system?

Questions.Subcategory: Is there a defined tech stack and/or set of frameworks?
Questions.Question: Is your platform built using your design system?

Questions.Subcategory: Is there a defined tech stack and/or set of frameworks?
Questions.Question: For your web applications are you using multiple frameworks?

Questions.Subcategory: Is there a defined tech stack and/or set of frameworks?
Questions.Question: Are you able to use the design system in all applications in your company?

Questions.Subcategory: Distribution & Version Control
Questions.Question: Is the design system isolated in its own repo or colocated with the product?

Questions.Subcategory: Distribution & Version Control
Questions.Question: Do you use a tool like a package manager to distribute the design system?

Questions.Subcategory: Distribution & Version Control
Questions.Question: Does your design system have its own release cadence?

Questions.Subcategory: Is there a defined communications platform?
Questions.Question: Is there a standard practice for using a central communication platform at the organization?
Questions.Description: Communication platforms in this context are chat tools like Slack and MS Teams, email tools like Outlook and other intracompany communication. Specifically we do not mean internal documentation like Sharepoint or Notion.

Questions.Subcategory: Is there a defined communications platform?
Questions.Question: In addition to communication platforms does your organization or team send out regular company updates from leadership?
Questions.Description: Company updates can be town halls, newsletters, webinars, quarterly reports etc.

Questions.Subcategory: Are all teams on the same design tooling?
Questions.Question: Are the designers using the same set of tools to do work?
Questions.Description: By set of tools we mean a specific set of tools and apps allowed by your company for official work.

Questions.Subcategory: Are all teams on the same design tooling?
Questions.Question: Is prototyping happening in your company?
Questions.Description: With prototyping we mean an artifact used to demonstrate interactivity and functionality to drive shared understanding.

Questions.Subcategory: Are all teams on the same design tooling?
Questions.Question: Does it happen in a code generating tool or static artifacts tool?

Questions.Subcategory: Are all teams on the same design tooling?
Questions.Question: Are design resources distributed through the same tool?
Questions.Description: Some organizations have specific places where logos, and images live that is sometimes separate from the libraries where components live, for example.

Questions.Subcategory: Quality & Testing
Questions.Question: Are there quality standards for your application for things like performance and accessibility?

Questions.Subcategory: Quality & Testing
Questions.Question: Are there defined quality guidelines that your application is evaluated by?

Questions.Subcategory: Quality & Testing
Questions.Question: Is your design system manually tested by a QA team?
